rationale: Report-fidelity lens, round 2. Both my round-1 asks are ADDRESSED. (1) The evidence citation now points to review/spec-107-approve/source-report.md, which exists in the worktree (13448 bytes) at the cited path; the nonexistent repo-review-2026-07-02.md reference is gone (grep returns nothing), and the path is consistent across Goal (line 10) and Background (line 14). (2) Attribution is now the exact three-plus-two framing I requested: three items from report §4 order 1-3 (dead-code sweep, setup managed-block unification, complete perf pair — verified against report §4 lines 90-92), plus two disclosed extras — guardrails restoration (R7) labeled 'a documentation-integrity fix beyond the report,' and spec-init alias dedup (R8) labeled 'the spec-init slice of the report's DRY #10 pulled forward' (report line 62 confirms DRY #10's first sub-item is exactly this). The renumbering to R1-R9 introduced no cross-reference drift: every R# in prose (Goal R7/R8, Impacted-Domains R7, ACs R5, Open-Qs R5/R7) resolves to the right requirement. The .golangci.yml sub-deferral of §4 item 1 is disclosed consistently in Background, In/Out of Scope, and ADR-0036. Transcription of retained items is unchanged and faithful. Remaining nit is info-only.

findings.detail: Round-1 ask 1 resolved. review/spec-107-approve/source-report.md now exists at the cited path (verified on disk, 13448 bytes) and is the report path cited in both Goal (line 10) and Background (line 14). The dangling repo-review-2026-07-02.md pointer is fully removed (grep for it returns nothing). A reader following the citation within the worktree lands on the real report, so the spec's 'no dangling documentation reference' goal is no longer self-violated.

Round-1 ask 2 resolved. Goal now reads 'three ... items from ... §4, order 1-3' (not four) plus 'two smaller fixes': guardrails (R7, 'a documentation-integrity fix beyond the report') and spec-init dedup (R8, 'the spec-init slice of the report's DRY #10 pulled forward'). This matches report §4 order 1-3 (dead-code sweep / setup unification / complete perf pair) and report DRY #10's first bullet exactly. Background line 14 echoes the same three-plus-two split. Attribution is now precise.

The deferral of the .golangci.yml carve-out removal to wave 2 dropped one requirement and renumbered to R1-R9. Verified internally consistent: requirement headers 1-9 are sequential and unique; every R# cross-reference in prose resolves correctly (Goal cites R7 guardrails + R8 spec-init; Impacted Domains line 30 cites R7 for AGENTS.md; ACs/Open-Qs cite R5; Out-of-Scope line 66 cites R1 for findRoot). No stale or dangling requirement number. The .golangci.yml sub-deferral (part of §4 item 1) is disclosed consistently in Background line 16, In-Scope line 56, Out-of-Scope line 66, Impacted-Domains line 30, ADR-0036 touchpoint, and Open Questions.

git ls-files shows source-report.md is not git-tracked (review/ is intentionally untracked per known follow-up adwu), while Background line 14 calls it 'the panel-local tracked copy.' 'panel-local' correctly signals it is a local-to-the-worktree copy, so the wording is defensible; 'tracked' is slightly imprecise but does not affect report fidelity or the citation's resolvability. Not a blocker — my round-1 ask explicitly accepted 'point at the actual path' as sufficient, which the spec did.

Re-confirmed at 18d7ea3f: the retained-item transcription is unchanged from round 1 and remains faithful — R1's dead-code list matches report §1 one-for-one (keeps live dirExists; defers only trace.Event.MarshalJSON with a stated adr-divergence-unowned rationale); R2-R4 match DRY #1 (setup unification + codex symlink fix + chainBeads fold, report line 51); R5-R6 match Perf #2+#3 / DRY #5 (report lines 79-80, 55). Wave-2 Out-of-Scope deferrals cover every remaining DRY/Perf item and the explicit non-findings. No invented scope.
